message.content:
{
  "analysis": "We have 190 full-frame OCR files and 190 bottom-crop OCR files. The ending OCR confirms the completed game has 262 moves, which is a hard validation target. No validated /app/solution.txt exists yet. Bottom OCR alone is too noisy/empty for many frames, so we should use full-frame OCR prompt histories and move-count progression to build and validate candidate moves before writing the final file.",
  "plan": "Run Panel 2's evidence-based pipeline: create a last-prompt log from full-frame OCR, create a candidate-move log, and inspect the candidate list and count. This is an intermediate validation step; no solution.txt will be written yet. We will iterate the extractor based on the observed candidate log until it is grounded and validated against 262 moves.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/work/build_last_prompt_log.py <<'PY'
import glob, os, re
fs=sorted(glob.glob('/app/work/ocr/frame_*.txt'), key=lambda p:int(os.path.basename(p).split('_')[1].split('.')[0]))
rows=[]
for idx,p in enumerate(fs,1):
    txt=open(p,errors='ignore').read()
    m=re.search(r'Moves?:\s*(\d+)', txt)
    mv=int(m.group(1)) if m else None
    lines=[l.rstrip() for l in txt.splitlines() if l.strip()]
    last=''
    for l in reversed(lines):
        if l.lstrip().startswith('>'):
            last=l.strip()
            break
    rows.append((idx,mv,last))
open('/app/work/logs/last_prompt.log','w').write('\n'.join(f'{r[0]:03d} mv={r[1]} last={r[2]!r}' for r in rows))
print('rows',len(rows))
PY
python3 /app/work/build_last_prompt_log.py
wc -l /app/work/logs/last_prompt.log
sed -n '1,100p' /app/work/logs/last_prompt.log
",
      "duration": 1.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "sed -n '101,190p' /app/work/logs/last_prompt.log
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/work/candidate_moves.py <<'PY'
import re
rows=[l.rstrip() for l in open('/app/work/logs/last_prompt.log')]
cmds=[]
prev=None
for line in rows:
    m=re.match(r'(\d{3}) mv=(\S+) last=(.*)', line)
    if not m: continue
    idx=int(m.group(1)); mv=m.group(2); last=eval(m.group(3))
    cur=last.lstrip('>').rstrip('._').strip()
    if not cur:
        continue
    if prev is None:
        prev=(idx,mv,cur)
        continue
    # if cur is a continuation of prev (typing), update
    if cur.startswith(prev[2]) or prev[2].endswith('_'):
        prev=(idx,mv,cur)
        continue
    # new command: commit previous
    cmds.append(prev)
    prev=(idx,mv,cur)
if prev:
    cmds.append(prev)
for i,(idx,mv,cur) in enumerate(cmds,1):
    print(f'{i:03d} f{idx:03d} mv={mv} {cur!r}')
print('count',len(cmds))
PY
python3 /app/work/candidate_moves.py > /app/work/logs/candidate_moves.log
wc -l /app/work/logs/candidate_moves.log
sed -n '1,120p' /app/work/logs/candidate_moves.log
",
      "duration": 1.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "sed -n '121,280p' /app/work/logs/candidate_moves.log
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}
